name: Solana JSON-RPC API
what_it_does: High-performance L1 blockchain JSON-RPC API for reading chain state, submitting transactions, querying accounts and programs, and subscribing to real-time events on the Solana network.
use_cases: Reading account balances, token holdings, and program state for DeFi agents
Submitting signed transactions for on-chain actions (swaps, transfers, staking)
Monitoring wallet activity and transaction history via RPC
Querying NFT ownership and metadata for marketplace automation agents
Subscribing to account change events for real-time portfolio monitoring
not_for: Traditional financial applications without blockchain use cases
Applications requiring strong finality guarantees (Solana has probabilistic finality)
Organizations with regulatory constraints prohibiting crypto asset interaction
best_when: An agent operates in the Solana DeFi ecosystem — reading on-chain state, submitting transactions, or monitoring program activity at high throughput.
avoid_when: You need an EVM-compatible blockchain — use Ethereum or Polygon APIs instead.

auth.notes: Public RPC nodes available without auth but rate-limited. For production use, dedicated RPC providers (Helius, QuickNode, Alchemy) require API keys. Public mainnet-beta and devnet/testnet nodes free but throttled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public RPC: free but heavily rate-limited (~100 req/10s)
pricing.paid_tiers: Helius: from $49/month; QuickNode: from $49/month; Alchemy: from $49/month
pricing.notes: Solana RPC itself is open-source and free. Production use requires dedicated RPC provider for reliability and rate limits. Multiple RPC providers with competitive pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Public: ~100 req/10s; Dedicated: varies by provider/plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC documentation is comprehensive on Solana docs. Error codes can be cryptic (0x1, 0x1771 program error codes). Transaction simulation helpful for pre-flight validation. WebSocket subscriptions for real-time events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transaction signatures are unique — same signed transaction can be resubmitted safely. Read operations are idempotent. State-changing transactions are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Transactions expire after ~90 seconds if not included — recent blockhash must be refreshed
Compute unit limits must be set correctly — transactions exceeding limits fail silently-ish
Preflight checks can be bypassed but this leads to failed on-chain transactions
WebSocket subscriptions drop — agents need reconnection logic for event monitoring

security_score_components.security_notes: Decentralized blockchain — no central authority. Private keys never transmitted to RPC. Transaction signing is client-side. RPC providers have their own security postures. Solana Labs publishes security advisories.
